mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.5, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real residential investment and new housing starts confirms a broad-based demand recovery that lifts cyclical equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year rolling window, or RSAFS and HOUST diverge without producing the predicted equity return dispersion.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, a sustained rise in the industrial production index indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not correlate with declining forward equity returns during identified late-cycle periods, or the negative return signal disappears when adjusted for FEDFUNDS tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly drives corporate earnings revisions, causing the S&P 500 to price in higher cash flows and generate positive forward returns within the subsequent week following each data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 revisions show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 10-year sample, or the direction of returns consistently opposes the sign of GDP surprises.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 2.5 percent annualized growth rate, it triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL breaches above the threshold do not precede negative 60-day forward returns, or equity multiples expand despite sustained inflation readings above the trigger level.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market normalization, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in walk-forward testing, or PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines without producing the expected equity risk premium compression.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts exceed 900 thousand units alongside rising real residential investment, it confirms a demand inflection that lifts construction and financial sector earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS jointly crossing the thresholds does not produce positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal generates false positives during periods of monetary tightening.
